content: I'm so excited for my next streaming event. It's going to be a 24-hour gaming marathon to raise money for a local children's hospital in Minneapolis.
That sounds like an amazing event. How did you get involved with the hospital, and what made you decide to support them with your streaming?

My kids, Emily and Jack, have been patients there, and the care they received was outstanding. I wanted to give back in a way that combines my passion for gaming with helping the community. My wife, Sarah, has been a huge help in organizing the event.
It's wonderful that you're using your platform for such a good cause. How do you plan to engage your viewers during the 24-hour marathon to keep them entertained and encourage donations?

I'll be playing a variety of games, including some new releases, and I've invited a few other streamers to join me for different segments. We'll also have giveaways and challenges to keep things interesting.
That sounds like a lot of fun. Have you set a specific fundraising goal for the event, and how will you be tracking donations during the stream?
We're aiming to raise $10,000, which would be a significant contribution to the hospital's new play therapy program. Donations will be tracked through a dedicated page on my streaming platform.
It's great that you have a clear goal in mind. What kind of support system do you have in place for managing the stress of streaming for 24 hours straight?

My family is very supportive, and I've also been training for a triathlon, which has helped me build up my endurance. I find that speedcubing is another great way to relax and challenge myself mentally.
I'm planning to take a short break after the marathon to focus on my gardening project. I've been wanting to start a small mushroom foraging initiative in our community garden.

Gardening and mushroom foraging can be very fulfilling. It's great that you're diversifying your hobbies and giving back to the community in different ways. How do you see your streaming and gaming community responding to your charity event?

I'm hopeful that they'll be very supportive. We've built a strong community over the years, and I know they'll rally behind a good cause. I'm planning to follow up the event with a pottery class, as a way to unwind and explore another creative interest.
It's wonderful that you have such a supportive community. Remember to take care of yourself during and after the event, especially with all the other projects you have lined up.
Definitely. I'm looking forward to the event and the opportunity to make a difference. And, of course, to spending more time with my family and pursuing my hobbies in Minneapolis.
It's great to see you balancing your work, family, and personal interests. I'm sure the event will be a success, and your community will appreciate the effort you're putting into it.
